dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.notReady: Tournament dashboard will appear here once the tournament is started.

onboarding.subtitle: Follow these steps to set up your first tournament

webhook.title: Configure webhook URL
webhook.secondaryMissing: Set the base URL for webhooks and demo uploads

servers.title: Add at least one CS2 server
servers.secondaryMissing: Start by adding your CS2 game servers

teamsPlayers.title: Create teams or add players
teamsPlayers.secondaryMissing: Create teams and/or add players with their Steam IDs

tournament.title: Configure your tournament
tournament.secondaryConfigured: Tournament configured with teams and format
tournament.secondaryMissing: Set up tournament type, format, teams, and maps

startCta.helper: Everything is ready! Review your configuration and start the tournament from the Tournament page.

stats.heading: Tournament Statistics

topPlayers.title: Top Players by ELO
topPlayers.subtitle: Global rating across all tournament types

eloDistribution.title: Player ELO Distribution
eloDistribution.subtitle: Number of players per Skill Rating band (bucket size {{bucket}}).

stats.noData: No data available. Create a tournament to see statistics.

settingsPage.title: Settings
settingsPage.intro: Configure core integrations, in-game defaults, and developer options.

webhook.title: MatchZy Webhook
webhook.description: MatchZy webhook URL that your CS2 servers can reach.
webhook.label: Webhook URL

steam.title: Steam Integration
steam.apiKeyHelper: Resolves Steam vanity URLs and player info. Leave blank to disable lookups.
steam.statusOk: Steam API key is set and reachable.
steam.statusNotConfigured: Steam API key is not set. Vanity URLs cannot be resolved until you add a key.
steam.statusUnreachable: Steam API key appears set but Steam could not be reached. Check your network or key.
steam.statusError: Failed to check Steam API status. Please try again.

mapSync.title: Map Synchronization
mapSync.description: Sync CS2 maps from the GitHub repository.
mapSync.success: Map sync completed! {{added}} new map(s) added, {{skipped}} already existed.
mapSync.rateLimit: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. Please try again in a few minutes. You can set GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable to increase the rate limit.
mapSync.rateLimitShort: GitHub API rate limit exceeded. Please try again in a few minutes.
mapSync.githubError: Unable to reach GitHub repository. Please check your internet connection and try again later.

chatDefaults.title: MatchZy Chat & Knife Defaults
chatDefaults.description: Configure in-game chat prefixes and default knife round behavior.
chatDefaults.chatPrefixHelper: Default: "[{Green}MAT{Default}]". Leave blank to reset to the default prefix.
chatDefaults.adminChatPrefixHelper: Default: "[ADMIN]". Leave blank to reset to the default admin prefix.
chatDefaults.knifeToggleLabel: Enable knife rounds by default for new matches (when sides are not pre-selected)
chatDefaults.knifeNote: Applies only when MatchZy would normally run a knife round; explicit side picks or shuffle-assigned sides are not changed.

ratings.title: Rating Updates
ratings.description: Control whether completed matches update player Skill Ratings. Disable this for events where an external system (for example your Excel sheet) is the source of truth for ratings.
ratings.toggleLabel: Update player ratings from completed matches
ratings.note: When disabled, all matches will still track stats (ADR, K/D, etc.), but player Skill Ratings will remain unchanged.

matchzyCore.title: MatchZy Core Defaults
matchzyCore.description: These defaults are sent to servers during initialization and before each match load.

expert.title: Expert settings
expert.description: These settings can make servers behave unexpectedly if misconfigured. Change them only if you know you need them.

ready.title: Ready / Match Start
ready.allowForceReady: Allow admins to force-ready players during setup (!forceready)

access.title: Server Access
access.kickWhenNoMatchLoaded: Kick/prevent joins when no match is loaded (tournament servers)
access.whitelistEnabledDefault: Enable whitelist by default (admins can toggle with .whitelist)

adminTools.title: Admin Tools
adminTools.pauseAfterRestore: Pause after round restore (.stop) so teams can coordinate

seriesEnd.title: Series End Timing
seriesEnd.kickDelayNoDemoLabel: Extra kick delay when demos are disabled (seconds)
seriesEnd.kickDelayDemoNoUploadLabel: Extra kick delay when demos enabled but no upload URL (seconds)
seriesEnd.kickDelayDemoUploadLabel: Extra kick delay when demos enabled and upload URL is set (seconds)

matchzyEnhanced.title: MatchZy Enhanced Features
matchzyEnhanced.description: Configure advanced match features. Leave settings empty to use tournament-type defaults.

autoready.title: Auto-Ready System
autoready.label: Auto-mark players as ready when they join
autoready.description: Players are automatically ready on join. They can still use .unready to opt-out.

sideSelection.title: Side Selection Timer
sideSelection.timeHelper: Time after knife round to choose sides

gg.title: Early Match Termination (.gg)
gg.minScoreDiffHelper: 0 = disabled. Example: 6 = only allow .gg when losing by 6+ rounds

ffw.title: Forfeit/Walkover System
ffw.enabled: Enable automatic forfeit when team disconnects
ffw.timeHelper: Time before forfeit is declared when entire team leaves

demo.title: Demo Recording
demo.description: When disabled, server restarts ~10s after match end (vs 60-90s with recording)

registration.title: Player registration
registration.description: Control whether new Steam logins automatically create player records. When disabled, only players created or imported by admins will appear in private tournaments and shuffle pools.
registration.toggleLabel: Allow anyone to register via Steam login
registration.recommendation: Recommended: keep this off for invite‑only or private tournaments so random Steam logins do not pollute the player list.

developer.title: Developer Options
developer.description: Local development helpers only; don't enable in production.
developer.simulateToggleLabel: Simulate matches (use bot-driven demo matches instead of real players)
developer.simulateNote: When enabled, generated MatchZy configs include "simulation": true for automated demo matches.
developer.timescaleDescription: Controls how fast simulated matches run. Higher values speed up bots and round flow. This only applies when simulation is enabled.
developer.timescaleWarning: Speeds above 2.0 can be a bit unstable and may cause odd bot behavior, but are fine to use for quick local testing.
developer.resetApiDescription: Danger zone: drops and recreates the entire database schema, then reinitializes default data (maps, map pools, etc.). Use only for local development. This is irreversible.
developer.resetApiSuccess: API and database reset successfully. All data has been wiped and defaults reinitialized.
developer.resetApiError: Failed to reset API and database. Check API logs for details.

resetApiDialog.title: Reset API & wipe all data?
resetApiDialog.description: This will drop and recreate the entire database schema. All tournaments, matches, players, teams, ratings, events, logs, and settings will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone. Continue?

debugChat.label: Enable MatchZy debug chat messages
debugChat.description: When enabled, MatchZy will print additional debug messages (like player connects, disconnects, and event traces) into in-game chat. Use this only for development and testing – not during live events.

resetDialog.title: Reset settings?
resetDialog.description: Reset all settings on this page back to defaults. Existing tournaments and servers are not changed, but new matches and lookups will use the default values. Continue?
